hypothesis: Market reference ETFs may be useful as context but weak as tradable candidates; removing SPY/QQQ/IWM from the trading universe could reduce slot competition and improve individual-stock alpha capture.

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull where strategy makes money but lags indexes
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with lower win rate

gate4_basis: Rejected because removing IWM regressed aggregate PnL by 4.76% and did not improve any EV window; removing SPY/QQQ alone was inert.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft-ranking remains sample-limited, so this tested deterministic universe pruning instead.

rejection_reason: IWM currently contributes useful tradable exposure in the accepted stack; SPY/QQQ are inert as candidates, so pruning market reference ETFs does not unlock alpha.
next_retry_requires: Do not remove IWM from the trading universe without new evidence that its candidate trades have degraded.
Do not spend another alpha cycle on SPY/QQQ candidate pruning unless they begin generating executed trades.
